title: Model Versioning and Change Control
objective: Ensure that every model promoted to production is uniquely versioned, traceable to its training data, evaluation results, and approval, and subject to controlled change-management with rollback capability.
rationale: Model changes can introduce regressions invisible to traditional change-control reviewers (a 2% accuracy drop on a protected subgroup, drift in calibration, new failure modes). Without versioning, organizations cannot reproduce a specific request's behavior, audit decision outcomes, or roll back when a regression is detected. EU AI Act Article 17 (quality management system) and ISO/IEC 42001 clause 8.3 require versioned, controlled change.
control_narrative: Every production model has a unique version identifier traceable to: training data snapshot (provenance records per AI-CTRL-002), training configuration, evaluation results (including fairness and adversarial robustness), approving authority, and deployment date. Model promotion to production follows a documented change-management flow with separation of duties between author and approver, mandatory pre-production evaluation gate (AI-CTRL-017), and rollback plan. Material change criteria are defined (e.g., > X% drift on key metric, new training data category, new tool/permission for agentic systems) and trigger re-evaluation. Customer-impacting model changes follow a notification cadence appropriate to the use case (e.g., enterprise tier of foundation models communicate model version with each response).

procedures: Obtain the Model Change Control standard and confirm scope, versioning scheme, and required traceability fields.
Confirm separation of duties between model author and approver.
Confirm pre-production evaluation gate is required prior to promotion.
Confirm material-change criteria are defined and trigger re-evaluation.
Confirm rollback capability is required as part of the deployment plan.
Confirm customer notification policy for model changes where applicable.
inquiries: Who approves model promotion to production?
How is rollback tested? How fast is it?
How are silent model changes from third-party providers detected (e.g., a SaaS LLM's underlying model changes)?
How are A/B or canary deployments governed?
inspections: Model registry sample showing versioning, lineage, and approvals.
Sample change records for sampled model promotions.
Rollback procedure documentation and test records.

procedures: From the inventory, sample production models promoted in the audit period.
For each sampled promotion, inspect the model registry entry and confirm: unique version, training-data lineage, evaluation results attached, approver identity, deployment date.
Confirm separation of duties was enforced (author ≠ approver).
Confirm pre-production evaluation results meet the documented thresholds.
For any production rollback in the audit period, confirm the rollback procedure was followed and root cause was investigated.
Inspect material-change re-evaluation records for any qualifying change.
For SaaS-foundation-model dependents, confirm vendor-notified model changes were reviewed and re-evaluated.

sample_size_guidance.population_type: Production model promotions in audit period
sample_size_guidance.high_risk: 25 promotions or 100% of EU AI Act high-risk system updates

reperformance_steps: For 1 sampled model, independently re-run a subset of the documented evaluation suite on the deployed version and compare to recorded results.

supporting.item: Vendor model change notifications and internal responses

evidence_requirements.retention_period: Lifetime of the model + 7 years after retirement (regulated); + 3 years (otherwise)

author: Emmanuel Guilherme Jr.

references.title: EU AI Act Article 17 — Quality management system
NIST AI RMF — MANAGE-2 Risk Management Actions
